segments.text: Apple pushed heavy glass design updates in iOS 26 are now dialing them back because users could not even read the
screens. Early adopters complained a lot of blurred text and headaches from all that frosted transparency. Notifications and menus blended into wallpapers,
making it difficult to see. Glass looks cool, but it forces your eyes to work harder. Study shows transparent
backgrounds reduces readability and increases fatigue, especially for those with vision issues. Apple added a
reduced transparency toggle in the later iOS to fix this, which is, you know, kind of like going back. User forums,
accessibility reports, and Apple's own design updates confirm this. Flashy effects often hurt readability. In the
end, best designs are the one that grow with you. Simple, clear and effortless.
Not the one that stray in your eye. If you like this, follow for more amazing design content.
